quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 10, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Comprehensive Cell Structure Review
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Organelle Functions, Plant vs. Animal Cells, Cell Theory, Unicellular Organisms

questions.question: What is the number of DNA molecules present in each chromosome?
questions.explanation: Each chromosome contains one long DNA molecule packaged with proteins. Before cell division, DNA replicates so each chromosome consists of two sister chromatids, but each chromatid still contains one DNA molecule.

questions.question: Animal cells
options: Are flexible and fixed in shape
Can vary their shape and have a large vacuole
Are rigid and have a cell wall
Are flexible and can vary their shape
questions.explanation: Animal cells lack a cell wall, making them flexible and able to change shape. They do not have a large central vacuole like plant cells.

questions.question: Which two structures in a plant cell provide support?
questions.explanation: The cell wall provides rigid structural support, while the large central vacuole maintains turgor pressure that pushes against the cell wall, keeping the cell firm.

questions.question: All the following are true about chloroplast EXCEPT:
options: It is the site of photosynthesis
It produces energy through cellular respiration
It is found in plant cells
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts perform photosynthesis, converting light energy to chemical energy. Cellular respiration occurs in mitochondria, not chloroplasts.

questions.question: Plant cells with many chloroplasts would be found in parts of a plant which:
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are sites of photosynthesis (food production). They are abundant in leaves and green stems where light exposure is highest.

questions.question: The cytoplasm of a cell:
options: Controls the activities within the cell
questions.explanation: Cytoplasm is the site of many metabolic reactions and provides a medium for organelles. The cell membrane acts as a barrier, the nucleus controls activities, and DNA stores information.

questions.question: Robert Hooke looked at a piece of cork under a microscope. The little boxes he saw in the cork are called —
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke coined the term 'cells' in 1665 after observing the box-like structures in cork. He was actually seeing only the cell walls of dead plant cells.

questions.question: Mitochondria
options: Convert sunlight into energy in animal cells
Convert sunlight into energy in plant cells
Release energy from food only in animal cells
Release energy from food in both plant and animal cells
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of all eukaryotic cells (both plant and animal), releasing energy from food through cellular respiration to produce ATP.

questions.question: The vacuole is:
options: A large sac-like structure in plant cells that regulates the water content of the cell
A disc-shaped structure containing chlorophyll for photosynthesis
A jelly-like material in which biochemical reactions occur in all living cells
A thin layer that controls entry and exit of materials to and from a cell
questions.explanation: Vacuoles are storage sacs. In plant cells, the large central vacuole stores water, nutrients, and waste, and helps maintain turgor pressure. Chloroplasts are disc-shaped, cytoplasm is jelly-like, and the cell membrane controls entry/exit.

questions.question: Which structure is present in all living cells?
questions.explanation: All living cells (prokaryotic and eukaryotic) have a cell membrane. Cell walls are absent in animal cells, chloroplasts only in plants/algae, and vacuoles vary.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of the cell wall?
questions.explanation: The cell wall provides structural support and protection. Photosynthesis occurs in chloroplasts, protein synthesis in ribosomes, and respiration in mitochondria.

questions.question: Which organelle gives green color to plant leaves?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll, the green pigment that captures light energy for photosynthesis.

questions.question: Select all that apply. Select the unicellular organisms:
questions.explanation: Euglena, Paramecium, Amoeba (protists) and Bacteria (prokaryotes) are unicellular. Volvox is colonial (many cells working together), and Earthworm is multicellular.

questions.question: What is the function of ribosomes in cells?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the sites of protein synthesis. Mitochondria produce energy, vacuoles store materials, and the nucleus controls cell activities.

questions.question: Which of the following is the principal structural element of a cell?
questions.explanation: Carbon is the principal structural element of organic molecules that make up cells (carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, nucleic acids). It forms the backbone of these molecules.

questions.question: Cell membrane is selectively permeable. This means that:
options: It allows all substances to pass freely
It allows some substances to pass while blocking others
It allows only water to pass through, while blocking other substances
It allows only saline substances to pass through
questions.explanation: Selective permeability means the cell membrane regulates what enters and exits, allowing some substances (like water, oxygen, nutrients) to pass while blocking others (like toxins, waste products).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a comprehensive understanding of cell biology.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review organelle functions and differences between cell types.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on key organelles and their roles in the cell.
scoreMessages.0: Please review cell structure, organelle functions, and cell type differences.
